feedback.text: Would be absolutely terrific for the Digital Fairness Act to also cover the cases when purchasing video games ensured that the game is playable even after the end of life scenario. Meaning that the video game should always have a single-player, offline mode that will guarantee it is playable even after the publisher/developer of the game are closing the servers and ending the support for online-only content. If the game is purchased, it should remain playable at the most basic level meaning: story/campaign is accessible, some form of local co-op if applicable, anything offline that does not tether the game to the developer/publisher will or their servers.
